name: Kindergarten Antiques & Material Culture — Semester 1
description: An 18-week, play-based Kindergarten pathway for old and new objects, family stories, and caring for belongings. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.
learningGoals: Build confidence and curiosity with old and new objects, family stories, and caring for belongings.
Communicate learning through talk, drawing, movement, play, or a simple creation.
Practice safe, kind, and responsible participation.

lessons.title: Notice and talk: old and new objects, family stories, and caring for belongings
lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about old and new objects, family stories, and caring for belongings through a close look at old and new objects, family stories, and caring for belongings.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about old and new objects, family stories, and caring for belongings through a guided practice with old and new objects, family stories, and caring for belongings.

units.title: Wonder and notice: old and new objects, family stories, and caring for belongings
units.essentialQuestion: What can we notice and wonder about old and new objects, family stories, and caring for belongings?
outcomes: I can notice details and share a question about old and new objects, family stories, and caring for belongings.
I can use words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate my thinking.

activities.title: A close look at old and new objects, family stories, and caring for belongings
activities.learnerAction: With an adult or teacher nearby when needed, the learner participates in a close look at old and new objects, family stories, and caring for belongings, uses words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate thinking, and cleans up safely.

successCriteria: The learner participates safely and respectfully in old and new objects, family stories, and caring for belongings.
The learner communicates one observation, choice, or question using speech, drawing, movement, or a supported response.
